quality_notes: Add vulnerability: mention a mistake, failure, or lesson learned. Try 'I was wrong', 'took me years to learn', or 'finally understood'.
Add more relevant emojis to improve scanability and engagement (target: 4 to 10).

Include one fenced visual block that matches the planned diagram type.

post_text:
📌 Platform Engineering

I'm stunned by GPT-5.6's ability to close a 30-year gap in convex optimization. OpenAI's latest model has achieved what many thought was impossible, and it's a wake-up call for all of us building AI systems.

GPT-5.6's impressive performance is a testament to the power of large language models in mathematical discovery.

This specific update matters to engineers like me building systems now because it highlights the potential of AI to accelerate mathematical progress. We often focus on applying existing techniques to new problems, but GPT-5.6 shows that AI can actually drive breakthroughs in fundamental math.

As I reflect on my own work with LG ThinQ and other smart home systems, I realize that the real challenge lies not in integrating AI into our products, but in harnessing its full potential to solve complex problems.

What mathematical problems are you trying to solve with your current AI stack, and how do you see GPT-5.6's achievement influencing your approach? 💬 Are you reevaluating your optimization strategies in light of this breakthrough?

#AIEngineering #LLM #PromptEngineering #GenerativeAI #GenAI
